figures.caption: Fig. 5.1. Convergence rates for smooth solutions. Top row: 2D results for p = 1.5 (left) and p = 3 (right). Bottom row: 3D results for p = 1.5 (left) and p = 3 (right).
Table 5.1 Total accumulated Newton iterations required to reach the target exponent p during the continuation process for smooth solutions under uniform refinement.

Fig. 5.2. Convergence rates for a singular right-hand side with p = 1.5 in 2D. Left: Uniform refinement from a standard coarse mesh. Center: Uniform refinement from a pre-adapted initial mesh. Right: Adaptive mesh refinement starting from a standard coarse mesh.

Fig. 5.3. Snapshots of the adaptively refined mesh in 2D for the singular problem (p = 1.5). From left to right: the initial coarse mesh, an early adapted mesh, and the mesh at the sixth refinement step.
Table 5.2 Case 2. Total accumulated Newton iterations required to reach the target exponent p = 1.5 for the singular problem in 2D.
